ankai: Client is asking for a third renewal or more, and your drain is already obvious
You were brought in to break a deadlock but are now being asked to do maintenance and operations
The site has become dependent on you personally, not on the methodology you left behind
Bad year overlaps with an Ankai contract — when exit timing arrives, leave without hesitation
yusui: Contract has passed six months and your fatigue is visible but the site has not noticed
Renewal terms are identical to the original contract without reflecting your drain
The site takes your contribution for granted and gives no proactive feedback
You feel like nothing is enough even though all KPIs are being met
kisei: Client questions your competence in the first month — this is a normal adjustment period reaction
Adjustment period consumed too many billable hours and contract margins are gone
Client expects full-speed output from day one with no room for adjustment
mei: Client is starting to question the need for an external consultant
Your deliverables are not meaningfully different from what the internal team could produce
Renewal motivation has shifted from professional value to mere capacity fill
gyotai: You are investing beyond contract scope without billing for the extra effort
Site personnel changed and all the verbal understandings you built are gone
Good chemistry led you to skip written documentation, and now there is no evidence when issues arise
